shock.adult_dose: Balanced Salt Solution (e.g., Ringer's Lactate): 500ml IV bolus
shock.notes_updates: Balanced Salt Solutions preferred over 0.9% Saline for septic shock to prevent hyperchloraemic acidosis.
0.9% Saline remains primary for hypovolaemia due to blood loss/dehydration.

inotropes.notes_updates: First-line vasopressor for septic shock. No longer restricted as 'Section 21'.
Second-line for septic shock if MAP targets not met with Noradrenaline.
Indicated for septic shock with cardiac dysfunction (persistent hypoperfusion despite fluids/vasopressors).
Largely superseded by Noradrenaline for shock.

resuscitation_medication.paediatric_dose: 10ug/kg IV OR 0.1ml/kg of 1:10 000 (1mg adrenaline/10ml normal saline)

resuscitation_medication.adult_dose: 300mg IV bolus over 3min (unstable pt); 150mg IV bolus over 3min (stable pt)

sedation.notes: Preferred for asthma/bronchospasm or trauma intubation

sedation.notes_updates: Avoid routine deep sedation; prioritize pain control and neuro-psychological support.
Adjust dose in obesity (lipophilic). Caution: Propofol Infusion Syndrome (PRIS).
Preferred for asthma/bronchospasm or trauma intubation.

seizures.adult_dose: 10mg IM or buccal (if no IV access)

seizures.notes_updates: WARNING: High teratogenic risk. Avoid in women of child-bearing potential unless acknowledgment of risk form is signed.
Safer alternative for women of child-bearing potential.
Monitor levels. Max infusion rate 1mg/kg/min (cardiac monitoring)
Use glass syringe; do not use plastic

analgesia.notes_updates: Encouraged as part of multimodal analgesia to spare opioids.
Avoid if renal impairment or GI bleeding
Used for PDA closure in neonates; monitor renal function

neuroprotective.notes_updates: Monitor osmolar gap; avoid if hypotensive
Alternative to mannitol for raised ICP
Barbiturate coma for refractory intracranial hypertension

broad_spectrum_antibiotics.notes_updates: Extended infusion: 3-4hr infusion. High dose for meningitis/CNS infection
Caution: seizure risk; reduce dose in renal failure

gram_positive.paediatric_dose: 15mg/kg IV 6hrly; Neonates: see dosing table by age

gram_positive.adult_dose: Loading: 400-800mg IV 12hrly x3; Maintenance: 400-800mg IV daily

aminoglycosides.adult_dose: 3-5mg/kg IV daily (once daily dosing); Traditional: 1-1.7mg/kg IV 8hrly

glycopeptides_and_lipopeptides.adult_dose: Loading: 9 MU IV; Maintenance: 4.5 MU IV 12hrly (adjust by renal function)
glycopeptides_and_lipopeptides.notes_updates: Monitor creatinine. CMAP (Colistin-Associated Acute Kidney Injury) risk. Reserve antibiotic

other_antibiotics.notes_updates: Monitor levels. Bone marrow suppression risk

antifungals.notes_updates: Monitor K+, Mg2+, creatinine. Pre-medication: paracetamol + hydrocortisone
Monitor LFTs, visual disturbances. Aspergillus drug of choice

antivirals.notes_updates: Adjust for renal function. Adequate hydration mandatory

antiparasitic.notes_updates: Superior to Quinine. Minimum 3 doses. If IV not available: rectal route

antiparasitic.adult_dose: Loading: 20mg/kg IV in 5% dextrose over 4h; Maintenance: 10mg/kg IV 8hrly

antituberculous.notes_updates: Give with pyridoxine to prevent neuropathy
Check visual acuity and colour vision

hiv_protocols.notes_updates: First-line adult ART. Tenofovir 300mg + Lamivudine 300mg + Dolutegravir 50mg

hiv_protocols.adult_dose: TLD + additional DTG 50mg 12hr after standard dose
hiv_protocols.notes_updates: Rifampicin lowers DTG levels - boost with extra 50mg dose

surgical_prophylaxis.notes_updates: Dose increased from 1g to 2g for adults (2024 update)

hyperkalaemia.notes: Shifts K+ into cells. Monitor glucose.

diuretics.notes: Monitor electrolytes. Ototoxicity at high doses
Monitor osmolar gap; avoid if hypotensive
Potassium-sparing; use with furosemide in ascites
Carbonic anhydrase inhibitor; causes metabolic acidosis

hyperkalaemia_management.notes: Drives K+ into cells. Monitor glucose q15min
Removes K+ from gut. Slow onset (several hours)

vitamins.notes: Prevention of Wernicke's encephalopathy. Give BEFORE glucose in malnourished/alcoholic patients
Give with INH to prevent neuropathy

gi_nutrition.notes: Prokinetic. Caution: extrapyramidal side effects in children
Bile acid binder; binds fat-soluble vitamins

protocols.protocol_dose: IV Protocol (3-bag): 1. 200 mg/kg in 500ml 5% Dex over 4 hrs, 2. 50 mg/kg in 500ml 5% Dex over 4 hrs, 3. 100 mg/kg in 1L 5% Dex over 16 hrs
For neurotoxic/cytotoxic bites. Consult poison center/specialist.
0.02 - 0.1 mg/kg/hr IV (Titrate to clearing of secretions).

reversal_agents.protocol_dose: 0.2mg IV over 15sec, then 0.1mg IV every 60sec (max 1mg). Repeat at 1min intervals if re-sedation occurs
0.4-2mg IV/IM (repeat every 2-3min to max 10mg). Infusion: 0.04-0.16mg/kg/hr IV
5-10mg IV bolus, then 1-5mg/hr infusion. High dose insulin + dextrose adjunct
0.05mg/kg IV with glycopyrrolate 0.01mg/kg IV (anticholinergic to prevent bradycardia)
1-2mg/kg IV over 5min (max 7mg/kg). Repeat in 1h if needed
May respond to naloxone 2-4mg IV. Supportive care primary

drug_withdrawal.protocol_dose: Diazepam 10-20mg IV q5-10min until sedation achieved. Thiamine 100mg IV first
Morphine: 0.04-0.2mg/kg PO q3-4h (titrated to Finnegan score)

formulae.formula: Na deficit = 0.6 x Wt(kg) x (Desired Na - Actual Na)

antihypertensives.notes_updates: Mixed alpha/beta blocker. Good for hypertensive emergencies
Ultra-short acting. Metabolised by RBC esterases

heart_failure_and_arrhythmia.adult_dose: Loading: 0.5-1mg IV/PO in divided doses; Maintenance: 125-250mcg daily
heart_failure_and_arrhythmia.notes_updates: Check levels. Toxicity: nausea, visual changes, arrhythmias

thrombolytics_and_anticoagulants.adult_dose: PE: 100mg IV over 2hrs (10mg bolus then 90mg). STEMI: 15mg bolus, then 50mg over 30min, then 35mg over 60min (max 100mg)

anticoagulation.adult_dose: Loading: 80units/kg IV; Maintenance: 18units/kg/hr IV (titrate to aPTT)
anticoagulation.notes_updates: Target aPTT: 50-70sec. Protamine sulfate for reversal (1mg per 100units heparin)
Anti-Xa monitoring in renal impairment, pregnancy, extremes of weight

anticoagulation.adult_dose: Loading: 5-10mg PO daily (adjust by INR); Maintenance: 2-5mg PO daily
anticoagulation.notes_updates: Target INR 2-3 (most indications). Vitamin K for reversal

products.notes: Transfuse over 2-4hrs. Monitor for transfusion reactions
For coagulopathy with active bleeding. Must be ABO compatible
Indicated: count <10 or <50 with active bleeding/invasive procedure

products.adult_dose: 500units IM (at different site to tetanus toxoid)
products.notes: For tetanus-prone wounds in non-immunised patients

steroids.adult_dose: Stress dose: 50-100mg IV 6-8hrly; Physiological: 20mg AM + 10mg PM
steroids.notes_updates: Stress dose for adrenal insufficiency, septic shock

endocrine.notes_updates: Check glucose hourly. Hold if K+ <3.3

endocrine.adult_dose: 50-100mcg SC/IV 8hrly; Variceal bleed: 50mcg IV bolus then 50mcg/hr

endocrine.notes_updates: DI, nocturnal enuresis, von Willebrand disease
Thyroid storm - use with carbimazole. Give 1h AFTER carbimazole

other.adult_dose: 1g IV over 10min (repeat 8hrly); Trauma: 1g over 10min then 1g over 8hrs

other.notes_updates: Malignant hyperthermia. Dissolve in sterile water
